chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P No.S-539 of 2023 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on M.A No.1207/2025 (XXII R 4) 2. For hearing of M.A No.92/2024 (Stay) 3. For hearing of main case 13.11.2025 Mr.Muhammad Arshad S. Pathan, Advocate for the Petitioner Mr.Wali Muhammad Jamari, A.A.G Sindh ***** On the last date of hearing, the son of respondent No.1, Muhammad Qasim Kamran Khan was present in Court seeking notice of his application under Order XXII Rule 4 C.P.C to implead the legal heirs of the Respondent/landlady (M.A No.1207/2025). He was fully aware of this matter being listed for hearing today. However, none has appeared on behalf of respondent No.1 and no intimation has been received. Meanwhile Syed Jan Muhammad Bukhari, Advocate, has appeared and requested time to enter appearance in this matter, claiming to represent Muhammad Ishaque and Muhammad Iqbal. At this stage, when the entire evidence has been recorded and the learned appellate forum has discussed such evidence of the trial Court in its appellate judgment and only limited scope for challenge the appellate judgment is available under writ jurisdiction, no ground is made out after almost two years from the date of institution of this petition for consideration of impleading Muhammad Ishaque and Muhammad Iqbal both sons of Muhammad Ahmed and both being strangers in this writ petition. Notwithstanding, that none filed application to implead the intervener nor produced them as witnesses during evidence. Petitioner counsel also contends that as respondent No.1 has passed away as recorded by this Court in its Order dated 31.10.2025 and as per the pending M.A No.1207/2025, hence neither further order can be passed without impleading the legal heirs of respondent No.1 nor any hearing can

Notwithstanding, that none filed application to implead the intervener nor produced them as witnesses during evidence. Petitioner counsel also contends that as respondent No.1 has passed away as recorded by this Court in its Order dated 31.10.2025 and as per the pending M.A No.1207/2025, hence neither further order can be passed without impleading the legal heirs of respondent No.1 nor any hearing can --2-- take place. This Court is bound to stay proceedings until the LRs are brought on record. Heard learned counsel for the petitioner and learned A.A.G, Sindh. For reasons to be recorded, the instant petition stands dismissed. JUDGE AHSAN K. ABRO
